message:
bd-2yjf: comprehensive unit tests for session.rs JSONL persistence + tree structure

Add 48 new unit tests covering session.rs edge cases:
- Session creation: header defaults, unique IDs, in_memory vs create_with_dir
- Message types: tool result, tool result error, bash execution, bash exclude_from_context,
  custom message, custom entry
- Parent linking: chain verification, model change/thinking level leaf updates
- Session name: get/set, latest-wins semantics
- Labels: add to existing, nonexistent target returns None
- JSONL round-trip: all message types, leaf ID, header fields, empty session
- Corruption recovery: middle entry, multiple entries, corrupted header fails
- Branching: nonexistent target, deep branching, sibling branches at fork, no fork
- Fork planning: user message, root message, nonexistent target, assistant message fails
- Compaction: truncates model context, omits old messages
- Large sessions: 500-entry append+path, 200-entry save+reload
- Entry ID generation: ensure_entry_ids fills missing, 8-char hex format
- Header mutation: set_model_header, set_branched_from
- HTML rendering: all message types present
- to_messages: includes only message entries, skips model_change/session_info
- JSONL format: header is first line, camelCase field names
- Error handling: nonexistent file, empty file

61 total tests pass in session::tests module (13 existing + 48 new).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1wc","title":"Implement CLI E2E integration tests with verbose logging","description":"# Implement CLI E2E integration tests with verbose logging

## Goal
Create end-to-end tests that invoke the pi binary directly and verify behavior,
with detailed logging for debugging failures.

## Background
src/main.rs (1581 lines) and src/cli.rs (332 lines) have minimal testing.
The CLI is the primary user interface and needs comprehensive E2E tests.

## Scope Boundary (Important)
- This bead builds the CLI E2E harness + offline CLI flag coverage.
- Provider-dependent scenarios live in:
  - bd-1ub (print mode + stdin)
  - bd-idw (session lifecycle)
  - bd-1o4 (tool enable/disable + error paths)

## Approach

### Test Harness
```rust
// tests/e2e_cli.rs

use std::process::{Command, Stdio};
use std::time::Instant;

struct CliTestHarness {
    binary_path: PathBuf,
    temp_dir: TempDir,
    env: HashMap<String, String>,
    logs: Vec<LogEntry>,
}

struct LogEntry {
    timestamp: Instant,
    level: LogLevel,
    message: String,
    context: HashMap<String, String>,
}

impl CliTestHarness {
    fn new() -> Self {
        let binary = cargo_bin("pi");
        let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
        Self {
            binary_path: binary,
            temp_dir: temp,
            env: HashMap::new(),
            logs: Vec::new(),
        }
    }

    fn log(&mut self, level: LogLevel, msg: &str, ctx: &[(&str, &str)]) {
        self.logs.push(LogEntry {
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            level,
            message: msg.to_string(),
            context: ctx.iter().map(|(k,v)| (k.to_string(), v.to_string())).collect(),
        });
    }

    fn run(&mut self, args: &[&str]) -> CliResult {
        self.log(Info, "Starting CLI", &[("args", &args.join(" "))]);

        let start = Instant::now();
        let output = Command::new(&self.binary_path)
            .args(args)
            .envs(&self.env)
            .current_dir(self.temp_dir.path())
            .output()
            .expect("Failed to run pi");

        let duration = start.elapsed();
        self.log(Info, "CLI completed", &[
            ("exit_code", &output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1).to_string()),
            ("duration_ms", &duration.as_millis().to_string()),
            ("stdout_len", &output.stdout.len().to_string()),
            ("stderr_len", &output.stderr.len().to_string()),
        ]);

        CliResult {
            exit_code: output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
            stdout: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
            stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
            duration,
        }
    }

    fn dump_logs(&self) -> String {
        // Format all logs for debugging
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) in the harness so failures auto-dump logs.
- Log: command, env (redacted), cwd, duration, stdout/stderr snapshot, and artifact paths.
- Attach logs + outputs as artifacts on failure (text snapshots).

## Determinism Requirements
- Offline-only tests here (help/version/list/config). Any provider-dependent test must live in sub-beads and use VCR playback.
- Ensure environment is fully controlled (PI_CONFIG_PATH, PI_SESSIONS_DIR).

## Test Categories (Offline)

### 1. Basic Invocation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_version_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--version"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("pi "));
    assert!(result.duration < Duration::from_millis(100));
}

#[test]
fn test_help_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--help"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("Usage:"));
}

#[test]
fn test_invalid_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--invalid-flag"]);
    assert_ne!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stderr.contains("error"));
}
```

### 2. Model Listing Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_models() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("claude"));
}

#[test]
fn test_list_models_filter() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models", "opus"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("opus"));
    assert!(!result.stdout.contains("sonnet"));
}
```

### 3. Subcommand Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_packages() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["list"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
}

#[test]
fn test_config_subcommand() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["config"]);
    // Verify output shows config paths
}
```

## Logging Format
```
[2026-02-02T10:30:45.123Z] INFO  Starting CLI
    args: --version
[2026-02-02T10:30:45.135Z] INFO  CLI completed
    exit_code: 0
    duration_ms: 12
    stdout_len: 23
    stderr_len: 0
--- STDOUT ---
pi 0.1.0
--- STDERR ---
(empty)
```

## Files
- tests/e2e_cli.rs
- tests/e2e_cli/harness.rs
- tests/e2e_cli/logging.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test harness with verbose logging
- [ ] Offline CLI flags tested (version/help/list/config)
- [ ] Logs dumped on test failure
- [ ] Tests run in <30 seconds total

 {"id":"bd-3v0j.4","title":"Terminal image rendering via Kitty graphics protocol and Sixel fallback","description":"# Goal
Render images directly in the terminal when the read tool returns image data, using Kitty graphics protocol (primary) with Sixel fallback, instead of showing "[image: 800x600, 45KB]" placeholder text.

# Background
The read tool (src/tools.rs) already supports reading images: it base64-encodes them and returns ImageContent blocks. The LLM can see these images. But the USER cannot — the TUI just shows a placeholder. Modern terminals (Kitty, iTerm2, WezTerm, Ghostty, foot) support inline image rendering.

# Terminal Protocol Support
1. Kitty Graphics Protocol (most capable):
   - Supported by: Kitty, WezTerm, Ghostty, Konsole 22+
   - Transmission: base64 chunks via escape sequences
   - Features: placement, scaling, animation
   - Detection: query escape sequence response

2. Sixel (widest legacy support):
   - Supported by: xterm (with flag), mlterm, foot, contour
   - Transmission: sixel-encoded pixel data
   - Lower quality than Kitty but more broadly supported

3. iTerm2 Inline Images:
   - Supported by: iTerm2 only
   - Transmission: base64 in OSC escape sequence
   - Detection: TERM_PROGRAM=iTerm.app

4. Fallback (unsupported terminals):
   - Show ASCII art approximation using Unicode block characters
   - Or just show the existing placeholder with dimensions

# Implementation Plan

1. Terminal capability detection:
   - Check TERM_PROGRAM, TERM, and query responses
   - Determine best protocol: Kitty > iTerm2 > Sixel > Fallback
   - Cache detection result for session lifetime

2. Image preparation:
   - Decode base64 image data
   - Resize to fit terminal width (maintain aspect ratio)
   - Use image crate (behind image-resize feature flag, already exists) for resize

3. Kitty protocol implementation:
   - Chunk base64 data into 4096-byte frames
   - Write APC escape sequences with transmission parameters
   - Set placement ID for cleanup on scroll

4. Sixel fallback:
   - Convert image to 256-color palette
   - Encode as Sixel escape sequence
   - Libraries: consider image-to-sixel pure Rust conversion

5. Integration with TUI:
   - In conversation viewport, detect ImageContent blocks
   - Render image inline at that position
   - Handle scroll: images re-render when scrolled into view
   - Handle resize: images re-render on terminal resize

# Performance
- Image decode/resize in background task (dont block event loop)
- Cache rendered escape sequences (dont re-encode on every frame)
- Lazy rendering: only render images visible in viewport

# Files to Modify
- New: src/terminal_images.rs (protocol detection, encoding, rendering)
- src/interactive.rs: Wire image rendering into viewport
- src/tui.rs: Image rendering for non-interactive mode

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Images render inline in Kitty terminal
- [ ] Sixel fallback works in supported terminals
- [ ] Placeholder text in unsupported terminals
- [ ] Images scale to terminal width
- [ ] Scroll works correctly with inline images
- [ ] No event loop blocking during image rendering
- [ ] Terminal capability detection cached","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:59:27.123429038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T09:03:29.208428635Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T09:03:29.208324601Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["images","rendering","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:59:27.123429038Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":530,"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Complete: Created src/terminal_images.rs with Kitty/iTerm2/fallback rendering.  {"id":"bd-3v0j.5","title":"Tool execution progress indicators (elapsed time, output count, file count)","description":"# Goal
Show meaningful progress indicators during long-running tool executions instead of just a generic spinner, giving users visibility into what is happening and how long it is taking.

# Background
Currently, when a tool executes (bash, grep, find), the TUI shows a spinner with text like "Running bash..." This gives no indication of progress, output volume, or whether the command is stuck. For commands that take 30-120 seconds, this is a poor user experience.

# Implementation Plan

1. Bash tool progress:
   - Show: "Running bash... 12s • 847 lines output"
   - Update elapsed time every second
   - Count output lines as they arrive (requires streaming tool output)
   - On timeout warning: "Running bash... 95s/120s • 2,341 lines ⚠️ timeout in 25s"

2. Grep tool progress:
   - Show: "Searching... 234 files scanned • 12 matches"
   - Parse ripgrep output for file count and match count
   - Update as results stream in

3. Find tool progress:
   - Show: "Finding files... 1,234 files found"
   - Count results as fd outputs them

4. Read tool progress:
   - Show: "Reading src/main.rs... 1,133 lines"
   - For large files: show size progress

5. Edit tool progress:
   - Show: "Editing src/main.rs... computing diff"
   - Brief, since edits are fast

# Architecture
- Modify the tool execution callback (on_update) to emit structured progress events
- The TUI spinner model receives progress events and updates display text
- Progress format: SpinnerText::Progress { tool, elapsed_secs, metrics: HashMap }
- Each tool type has its own metric formatting function

# Files to Modify
- src/tools.rs: Emit structured progress events from each tool
- src/interactive.rs: SpinnerModel parses progress events into display text
- src/tui.rs: Same for non-interactive print mode (optional)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bash tool shows elapsed time + output line count
- [ ] Grep tool shows files scanned + match count
- [ ] Find tool shows file count
- [ ] Updates at least once per second for long operations
- [ ] Timeout warning displayed when approaching limit
- [ ] No performance impact (progress events are lightweight)
- [ ] Spinner still works for tools with no streaming progress","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:59:42.301238654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:10.568081018Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:10.567989157Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tools","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:59:42.301238654Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":508,"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete. **src/interactive.rs**: Added ToolProgress struct. TUI ToolUpdate handler extracts progress from details JSON. Spinner rendering shows elapsed time and line count when >= 1s: 'Running bash (5s • 42 lines) ...' Progress is reset on ToolStart and ToolEnd.

**tests/tui_state.rs**: 3 new tests:
- tui_state_tool_update_with_progress_shows_elapsed_and_lines: verifies progress display

Quality gates: cargo check, clippy (0 new warnings), all 94 tui_state tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:36:47Z"},{"id":509,"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented tool execution progress indicators:

**Changes to src/interactive.rs:**
- Enhanced `ToolProgress` struct with `started_at: std::time::Instant` field for accurate elapsed time tracking from tool start (not just first update)
- Removed `Default` derive (replaced with explicit `new()` constructor)
- Added `ToolProgress::new()` - initializes with current timestamp
- Added `ToolProgress::update_from_details()` - parses `details.progress` JSON from tool callbacks, with wall-clock fallback
- Added `ToolProgress::format_display()` - formats compact status string like `Running bash · 3s · 42 lines`
- Added `format_count()` helper - K/M suffix formatting (1500 → 1.5K, 2500000 → 2.5M)
- **ToolStart handler**: Now creates `ToolProgress::new()` immediately (was `None`, only set on first update)
- **ToolUpdate handler**: Uses `update_from_details()` on existing progress (was creating new struct each time, losing start time)
- **View display**: Enhanced to show byte count (when no line count), timeout info, and K/M formatted counts

**Tests added (4 new):**
- `format_count_suffixes` - verifies K/M thresholds
- `tool_progress_format_display` - verifies output format with lines, bytes, timeout
- `tool_progress_update_from_details` - verifies JSON parsing

 {"id":"bd-3v0j.6","title":"Collapsible tool output blocks in conversation viewport","description":"# Goal
Add collapse/expand toggles to tool output blocks in the conversation viewport, so long tool results (grep with 200 matches, find with 1000 files) dont push important conversation content off-screen.

# Background
When a tool returns a large result (e.g., grep with 200 matches), the entire output is rendered inline in the conversation viewport. This pushes the AIs analysis and the users next input far off-screen, requiring extensive scrolling. In Claude Code and similar tools, tool outputs are collapsible — showing a summary line with an expand toggle.

# Implementation Plan

1. Tool output block structure:
   ▼ bash: cargo test (exit 0, 847 lines, 12.3s)    [collapse]
     running 90 tests
     test sse::tests::parse_basic ... ok
     test sse::tests::parse_multi ... ok
     ... (847 lines total)

   Collapsed:
   ▶ bash: cargo test (exit 0, 847 lines, 12.3s)    [expand]

2. Auto-collapse rules:
   - Tool output > 20 lines: auto-collapsed, show first 5 lines as preview
   - Tool output <= 20 lines: expanded by default
   - Configurable threshold in settings.json
   - User can always toggle manually

3. Interaction:
   - Click (or Enter when block focused) toggles collapse/expand
   - Keybinding: Tab cycles through collapsible blocks
   - Visual indicator: ▶ (collapsed) / ▼ (expanded) prefix
   - Summary line always visible: tool name, exit code, line count, duration

4. Implementation in viewport:
   - ViewportContent entries get a CollapsibleBlock variant
   - CollapsibleBlock { summary: String, content: String, is_collapsed: bool }
   - Viewport height calculation accounts for collapsed blocks (1 line vs full height)
   - Toggle updates viewport scroll position to keep context stable

5. Persistence:
   - Collapse state is session-transient (reset on session reload)
   - Not persisted to JSONL

# Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: CollapsibleBlock in viewport, toggle logic, keybinding
- May need viewport content model changes

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Large tool outputs auto-collapsed with preview
- [ ] Small tool outputs expanded by default
- [ ] Toggle works via keyboard
- [ ] Summary line shows tool name, exit code, duration, line count
- [ ] Viewport scroll position stable on toggle
- [ ] Configurable auto-collapse threshold
- [ ] Works with all 7 tool types","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:59:57.239953877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:13.213938732Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:13.213824659Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tui","ux","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:59:57.239953877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":518,"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN REFINEMENT (from review):

Collapsible blocks should apply to ALL large output blocks, not just tool outputs:
1. Tool outputs (bash, grep, find, read) — primary use case
2. Thinking blocks — extended thinking can be 500+ lines, equally disruptive
3. Code blocks in assistant responses — long code listings push context off-screen

The auto-collapse threshold should be per-block-type:
- Tool output: 20 lines (per settings, configurable)
- Thinking blocks: 10 lines (thinking is usually auxiliary)
- Code blocks: 30 lines (code needs more context to be useful)

Summary line format per type:
- Tool: "▶ bash: cargo test (exit 0, 847 lines, 12.3s)"
- Thinking: "▶ Thinking (245 lines, 3.2s)"
- Code: "▶ Code: rust (89 lines)"","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:38Z"},{"id":524,"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented collapsible tool output blocks in conversation viewport:

**Changes to src/interactive.rs:**

 {"id":"bd-3v0j.8","title":"Tests: TUI graduation feature suite — branching, diffs, images, progress, collapse","description":"# Goal
Comprehensive test suite for all new TUI features added in the graduation epic: session branching UI, inline diffs, image rendering, progress indicators, and collapsible blocks.

# Test Categories

## 1. Session Branching UI Tests (10+ tests)
State tests (tests/tui_state.rs pattern):
- Branch indicator shows "Branch 1/1" for unbranched session
- Branch indicator shows "Branch 2/3" when multiple branches exist
- Ctrl+B opens branch picker overlay
- Branch picker shows correct tree structure
- Enter in picker switches to selected branch
- Escape closes picker without switching
- Ctrl+Left/Right cycles between branches
- Branch creation from mid-conversation shows notification
- Branch picker handles 50+ branches without lag
- /branch command creates named branch

## 2. Inline Diff Rendering Tests (8+ tests)
- Single-line replacement shows word-level highlight
- Multi-line replacement shows line-level diff
- Added lines rendered in green
- Removed lines rendered in red
- Context lines rendered in gray
- Large diff (>50 lines) truncated with expand indicator
- Empty old text (pure addition) renders correctly
- Edit failure shows raw text (no diff attempted)

## 3. Image Rendering Tests (6+ tests)
- Kitty terminal detected → Kitty protocol used
- Non-Kitty terminal → Sixel attempted
- Unsupported terminal → placeholder text shown
- Image scales to terminal width
- Large image (4K) resized without blocking event loop
- Scroll past image and back → image re-renders

## 4. Progress Indicator Tests (6+ tests)
- Bash tool shows elapsed time (updates every second)
- Bash tool shows output line count
- Grep tool shows files scanned count
- Find tool shows files found count
- Timeout warning appears at 80% of limit
- Spinner fallback for tools without streaming progress

## 5. Collapsible Block Tests (8+ tests)
- Output >20 lines auto-collapsed with preview
- Output <=20 lines expanded by default
- Toggle expands collapsed block
- Toggle collapses expanded block
- Summary line shows tool name, exit code, duration, line count
- Viewport scroll position stable on toggle
- Tab cycles through collapsible blocks
- Configurable threshold honored (settings.json value)

## 6. Integration Tests (5+ tests)
- Full conversation with branching + diffs + collapse works end-to-end
- Thinking blocks also collapsible (if long)
- All features work together without rendering glitches
- PI_TEST_MODE=1 produces deterministic rendering
- Terminal resize re-renders all features correctly

}

 {"id":"bd-5yyc","title":"Task: Implement turn lifecycle events (turn_start, turn_end)","description":"# Task: Implement Turn Lifecycle Events

## Objective

Dispatch turn_start and turn_end events around each agent turn (provider call + tool execution cycle).

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
// turn_start
if (runner.hasHandlers('turn_start')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'turn_start',
        sessionId: session.id,
        turnIndex: context.turnIndex,
    });
}

// turn_end
if (runner.hasHandlers('turn_end')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'turn_end',
        sessionId: session.id,
        turnIndex: context.turnIndex,
        message: assistantMessage,
        toolResults: toolResults,
    });
}
```

## Events Covered

### turn_start Event
- **When**: Before calling provider.stream()
- **Purpose**: Observe/modify context, log turn start
- **Data**: session_id, turn_index

### turn_end Event
- **When**: After response and all tool results processed
- **Purpose**: Observe complete turn, log, analyze
- **Data**: session_id, turn_index, message (assistant), tool_results

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn run_turn(&self, turn_index: usize, context: &mut Context) -> Result<TurnResult> {
        // Dispatch turn_start
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("turn_start") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::TurnStart {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                    turn_index,
                };
                dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await?;
            }
        }
        
        // Call provider
        let response = self.provider.stream(/* ... */).await?;
        let assistant_message = self.process_response(response).await?;
        let tool_results = self.execute_tools(&assistant_message).await?;
        
        // Dispatch turn_end
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("turn_end") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::TurnEnd {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                    turn_index,
                    message: assistant_message.clone(),
                    tool_results: tool_results.clone(),
                };
                dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await?;
            }
        }
        
        Ok(TurnResult { message: assistant_message, tool_results })
    }
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (6 cases)
1. test_turn_start_fires_before_provider_call
2. test_turn_end_fires_after_processing
3. test_turn_index_increments_correctly
4. test_message_included_in_turn_end
5. test_tool_results_included_in_turn_end
6. test_handler_error_does_not_crash_agent

### E2E Test Script (tests/e2e/turn_events.sh)
Extension that logs turn timing with JSONL output.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-tg4w (EventDispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] turn_start dispatched before provider call
- [ ] turn_end dispatched after processing
- [ ] Event data correct
- [ ] Handler errors don't crash agent
- [ ] 6 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes with JSONL logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:50.117826523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:02:07.920087943Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:02:07.920022030Z","close_reason":"Turn lifecycle events (turn_start, turn_end) fully implemented in src/agent.rs.  {"id":"bd-pwz","title":"Implement SSE streaming adapter for asupersync","description":"# Implement SSE streaming adapter for asupersync

## Goal
Bridge asupersync HTTP streaming response to the existing SSE parser (src/sse.rs).

## Background
The SSE parser in src/sse.rs is already complete and well-tested:
- SseParser::new() creates parser
- SseParser::feed(&str) returns Vec<SseEvent>
- Handles: event types, data fields, IDs, comments, multiline data
- 9 unit tests covering all edge cases

The challenge is connecting asupersync's streaming response body to this parser.

## Implementation

### Streaming Adapter
```rust
// src/http/sse.rs (new file or extend existing)

use crate::sse::{SseParser, SseEvent};
use asupersync::Cx;

pub struct SseStream {
    parser: SseParser,
    body: BodyReader,
    buffer: String,
}

impl SseStream {
    pub fn new(response: Response) -> Self {
        Self {
            parser: SseParser::new(),
            body: response.into_body(),
            buffer: String::new(),
        }
    }
    
    pub async fn next(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Option<Result<SseEvent, Error>> {
        loop {
            // Try to parse buffered data first
            let events = self.parser.feed(&self.buffer);
            if !events.is_empty() {
                self.buffer.clear();
                return Some(Ok(events.remove(0)));
            }
            
            // Read more data from body stream
            match self.body.read_chunk(cx).await {
                Ok(Some(chunk)) => {
                    self.buffer.push_str(&String::from_utf8_lossy(&chunk));
                }
                Ok(None) => return None,  // Stream ended
                Err(e) => return Some(Err(e.into())),
            }
        }
    }
}

// Or implement as a proper Stream trait for use with async iteration
impl Stream for SseStream {
    type Item = Result<SseEvent, Error>;
    
    fn poll_next(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Option<Self::Item>> {
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Usage Pattern (in providers)
```rust
// In anthropic.rs
let response = client.post(url).json(&request).send(cx).await?;
let mut sse = SseStream::new(response);

while let Some(event) = sse.next(cx).await {
    match event? {
        SseEvent { data, event_type, .. } => {
            // Process Anthropic streaming event
        }
    }
}
```

## Considerations
1. **Backpressure**: Don't buffer entire response; process incrementally
2. **Error recovery**: Handle malformed events gracefully (log and continue)
3. **Cancellation**: Support clean shutdown via Cx cancellation
4. **Memory**: Limit buffer size to prevent OOM on malicious servers

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper must exist first)

## Testing
- Unit test: SseStream parses single event
- Unit test: SseStream handles multiline data
- Unit test: SseStream handles stream interruption
- Integration test: Stream from mock HTTP server

## Files
- src/http/sse.rs (new or extend)
- src/http/mod.rs (add re-export)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SseStream wraps Response and yields SseEvent
- [ ] Incremental parsing works (no full buffering)
- [ ] Cancellation handled cleanly
- [ ] Memory bounded

 {"id":"bd-rhl","title":"Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies","description":"# Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies

## Goal
Complete the HTTP migration by removing tokio and reqwest from Cargo.toml.

## Background
After all providers migrate to asupersync, these dependencies become unused:
- tokio (async runtime)
- tokio-stream (stream utilities)
- reqwest (HTTP client)

This reduces binary size and simplifies the dependency tree.

## Implementation Steps

1. **Update Cargo.toml**
```toml
# Remove these lines:
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-stream = "0.1"
reqwest = { version = "0.13", ... }

# Ensure asupersync is enabled (currently commented out):
asupersync = { path = "../asupersync", features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots", "sqlite"] }
```

2. **Update main.rs**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() { ... }

// After - use asupersync runtime
fn main() {
    asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new()
        .block_on(async_main())
}
```

3. **Update all async code**
- Replace tokio::spawn with asupersync::spawn
- Replace tokio::time::sleep with asupersync equivalents
- Replace tokio::select! with asupersync patterns
- Update channel usage (if tokio channels used)

4. **Update test harness**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }

// After
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }
```

## Files Affected
- Cargo.toml
- src/main.rs
- src/agent.rs (spawn, select)
- src/tools.rs (bash timeout)
- All test files

## Dependencies
- bd-37l (Anthropic migrated)
- bd-1vo (OpenAI migrated)
- bd-1iy (Gemini migrated)
- bd-ivj (Azure migrated)

## Testing
- cargo build must succeed without tokio
- All tests must pass
- Binary size should decrease (~500KB-1MB)

## Risks
- Some tokio-specific features may need alternatives
- Test harness changes may be extensive

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tokio not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] reqwest not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] cargo build succeeds
- [ ] All tests pass
